% Production-grid calibration pendant to Tab.~\ref{tab:calibration}.
% Generated by tools/assemble_production_metrics_table.py from the cached 112-init
% production intercomparison + per-baseline eval CSVs under
%   $STORE/baselines/{intercomparison,<model>/eval}/...
% CRPSS reproduces figures/headline_8way_table.tex to <0.005 (per_variable_crpss_table.py
% logic). SSR = per-pixel Fortin (M+1)/M (per-baseline ssr_line CSVs). SSIM/LSD/FSS from
% the per-baseline by-lead CSVs (= intercomparison combined CSVs to machine precision).
% W1 from per-baseline wd_kde wasserstein (global). IFS-ENS W1 = - (not in the re-eval; archive NaN gaps in every variable).
% ES/VS/SIGK at 120+240h from tools/submit_table_metrics_multilead.sh (job 2579121, 2026-06-21);
% bold = per-metric per-lead optimum over the eight rows.
% 7-variable mean (3D vars averaged over 500/850 hPa first; LSD over the 3D vars).

\begin{sidewaystable*}[p]
  \centering
  \scriptsize
  \setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
  \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.15}
  \caption{Production-grid (112 initialisations $\times$ 10 members, 2023-2024)
           multi-metric calibration for the seven production baselines plus the
           classical IFS-ENS reference, the deployment-scale pendant to
           Tab.~\ref{tab:calibration}. Two lead blocks (120, 240\,h); 7-variable
           mean (3D variables averaged over $p\in\{500,850\}$\,hPa first; LSD over
           the five 3D variables). Column arrows give each metric's optimum
           direction (SSR$^{\mathrm{pix}}$ targets 1; SIGK on its native unbounded scale, lower is better, comparable only within this table).
           \textbf{Bold}: per-metric per-lead optimum across the eight rows, decided before rounding. IFS-ENS
           is excluded from ES/VS/SIGK and $W_1$ (its WeatherBench-2 forecasts carry
           archive NaN gaps in every variable, leaving too few jointly-complete initialisations).}
